hero.headline: Better Auth vs WorkOS for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Better Auth and WorkOS — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Testing Better Auth vs WorkOS side by side reveals clear trade-offs. Better Auth integrates more naturally with App Router (8/10), while WorkOS counters with No free tier (enterprise sales). If you're optimizing for DX, Better Auth is the answer. If budget dominates, WorkOS holds its ground.
verdict_text: We recommend Better Auth for most Next.js App Router projects. In practice, its medium learning curve and 30 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. WorkOS is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need no free tier (enterprise sales) or prefer WorkOS's approach to authentication.

structured_data_points.label: Free Tier
structured_data_points.t1: Yes, fully open source with self-hosting

pros: Fully open source with no vendor lock-in or usage limits
Self-hosted on your own infrastructure - complete data control
Growing community and active development on GitHub
cons: Requires database setup and self-hosting infrastructure
Smaller ecosystem than Auth0 or Clerk
Fewer pre-built UI components compared to managed alternatives

pros: Enterprise-grade SSO with SAML/OIDC support out of the box
SCIM provisioning for enterprise directory integration
SOC 2 compliant and enterprise-ready from day one
Clean API design with excellent documentation
Used by Vercel, Airbnb, and other top-tier tech companies
cons: No free tier - requires sales conversation to get started
Not designed for consumer auth or B2C applications
Pricing is opaque and requires a sales call
Overkill for teams that don't need enterprise SSO

faq.question: Should I choose Better Auth or WorkOS for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Better Auth scores 8/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. WorkOS scores 5/10 with a Advanced learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Better Auth because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, fully open source with self-hosting or No free tier (enterprise sales) is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Better Auth or WorkOS more affordable?
faq.answer: Better Auth starts at Free (Open Source) with a free tier of Yes, fully open source with self-hosting. WorkOS starts at Custom pricing (enterprise) with a free tier of No free tier (enterprise sales). The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Better Auth offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Better Auth or WorkOS easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Better Auth has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 30 minutes. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve with an estimated setup time of 45 minutes. Better Auth is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Better Auth and WorkOS together?
faq.answer: Using both Better Auth and WorkOS in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Better Auth or WorkOS?
faq.answer: Better Auth has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 8/10, and a user rating of 4.5★. WorkOS has a Advanced learning curve, 5/10, and 4★. On paper, Better Auth leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
